items.title: Billboard’s Music Industry Events Calendar
items.summary: A complete rundown of the most important events across the music business.

items.title: Goose Announces Dates For 13th Annual Goosemas Holiday Shows
items.summary: This year's holiday celebration will take place on Dec. 11-12 at the historic Jim Whelan Boardwalk Hall in Atlantic City, N.J.

items.title: Melissa Auf Der Maur Announces First New Music In 16 Years, Hole Tease Something For Friday
items.summary: Melissa Auf der Maur, who has provided both her musical talents to iconic groups Hole and the Smashing Pumpkins, is releasing her first new music in 16 years. The Montreal-born musician and artist announced BASS WOMB ROOM: My ’90s 4-Track Demos x Field Recordings, which is out September 4 via Envision Records. The post Melissa Auf Der Maur Announces First New Music In 16 Years, Hole Tease Something For Friday appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Strawberry Hands Announces New Album A Jazz Blessing For The Animal: Hear “Dream Of Water”
items.summary: The Philadelphia musician Jacob Brunner is a member of Weyes Blood's touring band and he has played a supporting role on records from people like Karl Blau and Little Wings. Brunner also makes his own music under the name Strawberry Hands. Today, he announces plans to follow his 2020 album I Advance Wearing A Mask with a new one called A Jazz Blessing For The Animal.

items.title: Rustie – “Psion”
items.summary: In 2026, the massively influential Glaswegian producer Rustie is quietly emerging from years of silence, posting a series of mysterious new tracks on YouTube without announcing a record or making the new stuff available on streaming services. Thus far this year, Rustie has shared "Athanasia," "Amentet," "Trizkel Sun," and "Arora." Over the weekend, he dropped another one. The post Rustie – “Psion” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Rebecca Black announces tour for new LP ‘Age of the Exhibitionist’
items.summary: Her second album is out on September 4.

items.title: Hole’s Melissa Auf der Maur releasing unheard ’90s demos & new field recordings
items.summary: She also has a photo exhibition and accompanying photo book arriving in September.

items.title: Meg Lui announces Sufjan Stevens-produced debut LP, shares “Elvis Queen”
items.summary: ’Instant Validation’ also features contributions from Hannah Cohen, Sam Evian, Liam Kazar, and more.

items.title: BUTTHOLE SURFERS: Remember When Their Name Was Shocking?
items.summary: It’s September 23, 2025, at the Egyptian Theater in Hollywood, where a tornado of psychedelic noise and insanity has descended, brought to delirious life by the reunited Butthole Surfers. It’s their first performance in eight years, and the opening song is “Cherub,” a frazzled bit of feedback and art-punk melody from 1984. Singer Gibby Haynes […]

items.title: Brad Paisley defends marriage of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce: “One of the least pretentious weddings that I’ve ever seen or ever will see”
items.summary: The country singer invited Swift to be the opening act of his tour back in 2007 The post Brad Paisley defends marriage of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce: “One of the least pretentious weddings that I’ve ever seen or ever will see” appeared first on NME.

items.title: Arca announces new album ‘XXXXX’ and ambitious Daniel Sannwald-directed livestream
items.summary: The Venezuelan musician's new album drops July 31 and is said to be "darker, more aggressive, more experimental" The post Arca announces new album ‘XXXXX’ and ambitious Daniel Sannwald-directed livestream appeared first on NME.

items.title: Government announce £45million to “turn up” music industry for artists, businesses and children – but what do Andy Burnham’s government need to do now?
items.summary: MPs and industry insiders speak out on progress being made, and urgent, vital action still needed on the ticket levy for protecting artists and venues, as well as beating the Brexit touring crisis and banning ticket touts The post Government announce £45million to “turn up” music industry for artists, businesses and children – but what do Andy Burnham’s government need to do now? appeared first on NME.
